narrative:
Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away feels like a door slamming shut just as you reach for it. Our prediction model looked at your profile and foresaw a 73% chance of struggling to pass the bar exam down the line, painting a picture of hurdles that, together, made the path ahead seem steeper than we could endorse right now. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, the strongest signal pulling toward this outcome, as it showed your grades placed you at the bottom of your class during that critical later stage. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 1.0, reinforcing a pattern of early challenges that didn't shift much over time. Your LSAT score of 27 added weight to those concerns, signaling to the model that foundational skills for legal reasoning might need more strengthening. Even with your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.3 and upper-middle family income, these factors nudged the prediction further toward difficulty, perhaps reflecting resources that haven't yet translated into academic momentum. Your Asian background, full-time study plans, and gender also played roles in this composite story, each tipping the scales slightly against success based on patterns the model has seen. It's not that any one thing defines you—it's this blend that worried us, like ingredients in a recipe that just didn't rise as hoped. This isn't the end of your story; many have rewritten theirs by targeting those early deciles or LSAT hurdles head-on. We truly see your potential and wish you strength as you chart the next steps. With encouragement,  
The Admissions Team
